data.narrative:
ARX Robotics is a Series A defense and security robotics company founded by Marc Wietfeld that has raised $106M+ to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 10 total peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—with focus on 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two valuation methods apply directly. Stage-progression modeling uses the $106M raised as a proxy baseline, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $212M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 comparable Series A defense firms produces a median valuation of $225M, with the peer set ranging from $125M to $500M. These methods triangulate to a low of $180M, a median of $218M, and a high of $259M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of approximately $180M–$259M, with a central point near $218M. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility and near-term revenue growth relative to peer cohorts, as well as the breadth of differentiation in robotics capabilities versus Stark Defence and Vatn Systems, which command substantially higher multiples within the peer set.
